title: Self-Observation Begins
focus: Notice what drains and energises. No fixing yet.
note: Three sentences every evening. That is all.

title: Energy Mapping
focus: Track where energy goes and comes from across a full week.

title: Instinct & Fairness
focus: Start trusting your own data. Name what happened to you honestly.
note: The instinct log and fairness inventory run together this week.

title: Reading the Room
focus: Map the real structure of your workplace. Not the org chart.

title: Visibility Begins
focus: One sentence in one meeting. That is the whole task.
note: Prepare it before you go in. Say it. Stop.

title: Stillness Practice
focus: Physical stillness under pressure is trainable.
note: One moment per day. Feet flat, hands still.

title: Values — Real Ones
focus: Build your internal anchor from evidence, not aspiration.
note: What did you choose when it actually cost you something?

title: Small Bets
focus: Act on one instinct per week before you talk yourself out of it.
note: Low stakes. Just prove to yourself you can do it.

title: Anchor Under Pressure
focus: Use your values list when something pulls you off centre.
note: Return to the list. What does it say about this situation?

title: Presence Walk
focus: Walk into a difficult room deliberately. Occupy your actual space.
note: Where you sit. How you move. This is physical and trainable.

title: Rule Audit
focus: Which rules are load-bearing. Which are discretionary.
note: Understanding a system is not becoming it.

title: The Non-Reaction
focus: One provocation. Pause one second longer than natural.
note: Respond to content not tone. Give them nothing to work with.

title: Speaking With Authority
focus: Say the important thing clearly and stop. No justification needed.
note: One sentence. Then silence. Let it land.

title: No-Fear Audit
focus: Name what you are avoiding. Examine what you think will happen.
note: What is the realistic probability. Could you survive it if it did.

title: Integration
focus: Everything from Phase 1 and 2 in a live situation.
note: You have the tools. Use them together.

title: Final Week
focus: The no-fear audit one last time. What has shifted.
note: Note what is different. This is your baseline going forward.
